user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks damages for defamation against a Defendant for publishing false and malicious allegations on Facebook that damaged the Plaintiff's business reputation. The suit includes claims for loss of goodwill and mental agony, seeking Rs. 10,00,000 in damages and a permanent injunction against further publication.

draft_artifacts.title: PLAINT IN A SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION (LIBEL) AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION (FACEBOOK / SOCIAL MEDIA PUBLICATION)
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE LEARNED DISTRICT JUDGE, {{DISTRICT_NAME}}, {{STATE_NAME}}, INDIA
(Civil Original Jurisdiction)

Civil Suit No.: {{SUIT_NUMBER}} of {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Age: {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing / Having place of business at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},
                                                                     ...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Age: {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing / Having place of business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}},
                                                                     ...Defendant

PLAINT UNDER THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION (LIBEL) AMOUNTING TO RS. 10,00,000/- (RUPEES TEN LAKHS ONLY) AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION RESTRAINING FURTHER PUBLICATION ON FACEBOOK OR ANY OTHER SOCIAL MEDIA PLATFORM

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. Parties
   1.1. The Plaintiff is an individual / entity engaged in {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION}} and enjoys goodwill and reputation in the market and amongst customers, vendors, and the general public.
   1.2. The Defendant is an individual who is active on the social media platform Facebook and is responsible for the acts complained of herein.

2. Jurisdiction
   2.1. This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court, inter alia, because the impugned Facebook publication(s) were accessible, read, and acted upon by third parties within {{DISTRICT_NAME}}, and the Plaintiff’s business reputation and goodwill were harmed within the said jurisdiction.
   2.2. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the value of the suit for the purposes of jurisdiction is Rs. 10,00,000/-.

3. Facts of the case
   3.1. The Plaintiff states that on or about {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} (and/or on {{DATE_OF_KNOWLEDGE}}), the Defendant published on Facebook a post / content concerning the Plaintiff, containing false, baseless, and malicious allegations.
   3.2. The Defendant’s publication was made intentionally and with malice, with the object of lowering the Plaintiff’s reputation in the estimation of customers, business associates, and members of the public, and to cause loss to the Plaintiff’s business.
   3.3. The Plaintiff states that there is no truth in the Defendant’s allegations. The Defendant made the impugned statements without any reasonable basis, without verifying facts, and with reckless disregard to the truth.

4. Defamatory statements (verbatim reproduction)
   4.1. The Plaintiff reproduces the impugned defamatory statements as published by the Defendant on Facebook (as captured in screenshot(s)/printout(s)) below:

       (A) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_1}}”
       (B) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_2}}”
       (C) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_3}}”

   4.2. The aforesaid statements are per se defamatory and are calculated to injure the Plaintiff’s business reputation, trade standing, and goodwill. The statements convey to ordinary readers that the Plaintiff is dishonest / untrustworthy / engaged in wrongful conduct in business, thereby lowering the Plaintiff’s reputation.

5. Publication to third parties
   5.1. The Defendant published the defamatory statements on Facebook, a platform intended for and enabling dissemination to third parties.
   5.2. The impugned post(s) were accessible to, and read by, third persons including (without limitation) customers, prospective customers, vendors, and other members of the public.
   5.3. In an action for libel, particulars of the publication and the persons to whom the libel was published are material. Accordingly, the Plaintiff states that the impugned post(s) were published to and seen by persons including:
       (i) {{THIRD_PARTY_PERSON_1_NAME}};
       (ii) {{THIRD_PARTY_PERSON_2_NAME}};
       (iii) {{THIRD_PARTY_PERSON_3_NAME}};
       and/or other Facebook users within the Plaintiff’s circle and the general public.

6. Malice
   6.1. The Defendant acted with express malice. The Defendant knew the statements to be false and nevertheless published them, or in any case published them with reckless disregard to their truth.
   6.2. The Defendant’s choice of a mass-dissemination platform (Facebook), the language used, and the timing and manner of publication demonstrate an intent to harm the Plaintiff’s business reputation and to cause the Plaintiff mental distress.

7. Loss of goodwill, business harm, and mental agony
   7.1. Due to the Defendant’s false and malicious Facebook publication, the Plaintiff has suffered and continues to suffer:
       (a) loss of goodwill and reputation in business;
       (b) loss of customer confidence and adverse impact on business relations;
       (c) humiliation and lowering of social and professional standing;
       (d) mental agony, stress, and emotional distress.
   7.2. The Plaintiff states that the nature of the allegations and the wide reach of Facebook have aggravated the damage. The defamatory statements continue to harm the Plaintiff so long as the post(s) remain available and/or are capable of being shared, reposted, or otherwise disseminated.

8. Cause of action
   8.1. The cause of action first arose on {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} when the Defendant published the defamatory statements on Facebook.
   8.2. The right to sue accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} when the Plaintiff’s reputation and business goodwill were harmed and the Plaintiff suffered injury by reason of publication to third parties.
   8.3. The cause of action is of a continuing nature as the impugned content continues to remain accessible and capable of further dissemination on Facebook and/or other platforms, thereby causing continuing injury to the Plaintiff.

9. Limitation
   9.1. The present suit is filed within limitation. Under Article 75 of the Schedule (Part VII—Suits Relating to Tort) providing limitation for “compensation for libel” as one year “when the libel is published”, the limitation period is one year from the date of publication.
   9.2. The Defendant published the impugned libel on {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}. The present suit is filed on {{DATE_OF_INSTITUTION}} and is therefore within the prescribed period.
   9.3. Without prejudice, if the publication is found to be repeated / republished / continuing by continued online availability and/or re-sharing, the Plaintiff craves leave to urge such facts as may be necessary at trial on the basis of evidence.

10. Valuation and Court Fee
   10.1. For the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 10,00,000/- being the amount of damages claimed.
   10.2. Court fee: {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

11. No other proceedings
   11.1. The Plaintiff states that no other suit between the same parties on the same cause of action for the same reliefs is pending before any court, to the best of the Plaintiff’s knowledge.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

A. Pass a decree for damages/compensation in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only) towards loss of goodwill, harm to business reputation, and mental agony caused by the Defendant’s defamatory Facebook publication;

B. Pass a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, representatives, and all persons acting through or under him from publishing, republishing, sharing, circulating, uploading, or otherwise disseminating the impugned defamatory statements and/or any other defamatory content concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform;

C. Direct the Defendant to remove / take down the impugned defamatory post(s)/content from Facebook and to desist from further publication;

D. Award costs of the suit in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant; and

E. Pass such other and further order(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

draft_artifacts.title: PLAINT IN A SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION (LIBEL) AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION (FACEBOOK / SOCIAL MEDIA PUBLICATION)
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE LEARNED DISTRICT JUDGE, {{DISTRICT_NAME}}, {{STATE_NAME}}, INDIA
(Civil Original Jurisdiction)

Civil Suit No.: {{SUIT_NUMBER}} of {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Age: {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing / Having place of business at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},
                                                                     ...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Age: {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing / Having place of business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}},
                                                                     ...Defendant

PLAINT UNDER THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION (LIBEL) AMOUNTING TO RS. 10,00,000/- (RUPEES TEN LAKHS ONLY) AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION RESTRAINING FURTHER PUBLICATION ON FACEBOOK OR ANY OTHER SOCIAL MEDIA PLATFORM

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. Parties
   1.1. The Plaintiff is an individual / entity engaged in {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION}} and enjoys goodwill and reputation in the market and amongst customers, vendors, and the general public.
   1.2. The Defendant is an individual who is active on the social media platform Facebook and is responsible for the acts complained of herein.

2. Jurisdiction
   2.1. This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court, inter alia, because the impugned Facebook publication(s) were accessible, read, and acted upon by third parties within {{DISTRICT_NAME}}, and the Plaintiff’s business reputation and goodwill were harmed within the said jurisdiction.
   2.2. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the value of the suit for the purposes of jurisdiction is Rs. 10,00,000/-.

3. Facts of the case
   3.1. The Plaintiff states that on or about {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} (and/or on {{DATE_OF_KNOWLEDGE}}), the Defendant published on Facebook a post / content concerning the Plaintiff, containing false, baseless, and malicious allegations.
   3.2. The Defendant’s publication was made intentionally and with malice, with the object of lowering the Plaintiff’s reputation in the estimation of customers, business associates, and members of the public, and to cause loss to the Plaintiff’s business.
   3.3. The Plaintiff states that there is no truth in the Defendant’s allegations. The Defendant made the impugned statements without any reasonable basis, without verifying facts, and with reckless disregard to the truth.

4. Defamatory statements (verbatim reproduction)
   4.1. The Plaintiff reproduces the impugned defamatory statements as published by the Defendant on Facebook (as captured in screenshot(s)/printout(s)) below:

       (A) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_1}}”
       (B) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_2}}”
       (C) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_3}}”

   4.2. The aforesaid statements are per se defamatory and are calculated to injure the Plaintiff’s business reputation, trade standing, and goodwill. The statements convey to ordinary readers that the Plaintiff is dishonest / untrustworthy / engaged in wrongful conduct in business, thereby lowering the Plaintiff’s reputation.

5. Publication to third parties
   5.1. The Defendant published the defamatory statements on Facebook, a platform intended for and enabling dissemination to third parties.
   5.2. The impugned post(s) were accessible to, and read by, third persons including (without limitation) customers, prospective customers, vendors, and other members of the public.
   5.3. In an action for libel, particulars of the publication and the persons to whom the libel was published are material. Accordingly, the Plaintiff states that the impugned post(s) were published to and seen by persons including:
       (i) {{THIRD_PARTY_PERSON_1_NAME}};
       (ii) {{THIRD_PARTY_PERSON_2_NAME}};
       (iii) {{THIRD_PARTY_PERSON_3_NAME}};
       and/or other Facebook users within the Plaintiff’s circle and the general public.

6. Malice
   6.1. The Defendant acted with express malice. The Defendant knew the statements to be false and nevertheless published them, or in any case published them with reckless disregard to their truth.
   6.2. The Defendant’s choice of a mass-dissemination platform (Facebook), the language used, and the timing and manner of publication demonstrate an intent to harm the Plaintiff’s business reputation and to cause the Plaintiff mental distress.

7. Loss of goodwill, business harm, and mental agony
   7.1. Due to the Defendant’s false and malicious Facebook publication, the Plaintiff has suffered and continues to suffer:
       (a) loss of goodwill and reputation in business;
       (b) loss of customer confidence and adverse impact on business relations;
       (c) humiliation and lowering of social and professional standing;
       (d) mental agony, stress, and emotional distress.
   7.2. The Plaintiff states that the nature of the allegations and the wide reach of Facebook have aggravated the damage. The defamatory statements continue to harm the Plaintiff so long as the post(s) remain available and/or are capable of being shared, reposted, or otherwise disseminated.

8. Cause of action
   8.1. The cause of action first arose on {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} when the Defendant published the defamatory statements on Facebook.
   8.2. The right to sue accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} when the Plaintiff’s reputation and business goodwill were harmed and the Plaintiff suffered injury by reason of publication to third parties.
   8.3. The cause of action is of a continuing nature as the impugned content continues to remain accessible and capable of further dissemination on Facebook and/or other platforms, thereby causing continuing injury to the Plaintiff.

9. Limitation
   9.1. The present suit is filed within limitation. The limitation provision applicable to a civil suit seeking compensation/damages for libel/defamation under the Limitation Act, 1963 is: {{LIMITATION_PROVISION_FOR_DEFAMATION}}.
   9.2. On the Plaintiff’s case, the limitation period runs from the date of publication of the impugned Facebook content i.e., {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} (and/or from the relevant accrual date, as applicable in law on the facts). The present suit is filed on {{DATE_OF_INSTITUTION}} and is therefore within limitation.
   9.3. Without prejudice, if the publication is found to be repeated / republished / continuing by continued online availability and/or re-sharing, the Plaintiff craves leave to urge such facts as may be necessary at trial on the basis of evidence.

10. Valuation and Court Fee
   10.1. For the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 10,00,000/- being the amount of damages claimed.
   10.2. Court fee: {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

11. No other proceedings
   11.1. The Plaintiff states that no other suit between the same parties on the same cause of action for the same reliefs is pending before any court, to the best of the Plaintiff’s knowledge.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

A. Pass a decree for damages/compensation in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only) towards loss of goodwill, harm to business reputation, and mental agony caused by the Defendant’s defamatory Facebook publication;

B. Pass a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, representatives, and all persons acting through or under him from publishing, republishing, sharing, circulating, uploading, or otherwise disseminating the impugned defamatory statements and/or any other defamatory content concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform;

C. Direct the Defendant to remove / take down the impugned defamatory post(s)/content from Facebook and to desist from further publication;

D. Award costs of the suit in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant; and

E. Pass such other and further order(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ANNEXURES

Annexure P-1: Screenshot(s) / printout(s) of the Defendant’s Facebook post(s) containing the defamatory statements (electronic record) (with metadata/details, as available).
Annexure P-2: URL/link details of the Facebook post(s) (if available) and date/time of access.
Annexure P-3: Proof of Plaintiff’s business identity and goodwill ({{DESCRIPTION_OF_BUSINESS_DOCUMENTS}}).
Annexure P-4: Any customer/vendor communications reflecting impact, if available ({{DESCRIPTION_OF_IMPACT_DOCUMENTS}}).

Note on electronic evidence: The Plaintiff relies upon electronic records (screenshots/printouts/URLs). Any certification requirement applicable to such electronic records shall be complied with at the time of filing/leading evidence, as advised.

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to {{PARA_FACTS_END}} are true and correct to my knowledge and belief, and those of paragraphs {{PARA_LEGAL_START}} to {{PARA_LEGAL_END}} are based on legal advice and believed to be true. Nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{PLACE_NAME}} on this {{DAY}} day of {{MONTH}}, {{YEAR}}.


_________________________
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
Plaintiff

Through Counsel:
{{COUNSEL_NAME}}
{{COUNSEL_ENROLMENT_NO}}
{{COUNSEL_ADDRESS}}
{{COUNSEL_PHONE_EMAIL}}
